taxonomy: Pombalia parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Brazil and Paraguay. The plant belongs to the genus Pombalia, which is closely related to the genus Rinorea. Pombalia parviflora is a small, herbaceous plant with small flowers and is often found in humid, forested areas. The classification of Pombalia parviflora is part of the broader systematics of the Violaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the violet family.
